meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding: Solve 'No Project Ideas' for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project stagnation. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led tutorial.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap vanishes when you focus on high-impact infrastructure rather than novel product concepts. By building a sophisticated, modular user onboarding flow, you solve a universal SaaS problem, demonstrate mastery of state management and UX psychology, and create a reusable component that adds immediate value to any portfolio.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new repository is the graveyard of engineering ambition. You have the skills, you have the hunger, but you lack the 'North Star' project that bridges the gap between tutorial completion and production-grade engineering. Most students waste months chasing the 'next big app' while ignoring the silent killer of every SaaS startup: user churn during the first five minutes.

Today, we pivot. Instead of building a generic To-Do app, you will engineer a professional-grade user onboarding system. This isn't just a feature; it is the gatekeeper of user retention. By mastering the orchestration of state, data collection, and progressive disclosure, you transform from a student into a builder who understands the mechanics of growth.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Structure your onboarding as a finite state machine to ensure robust flow control and avoid spaghetti code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a state machine for a multi-step user onboarding flow. Include states for 'welcome', 'profile_setup', 'preferences', and 'completion'. Provide the schema for state transitions and validation logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize a TypeScript state machine for onboarding using XState patterns.'

steps.title: Build Dynamic Form Components
steps.description: Develop reusable, schema-driven input components that handle validation and error states gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable form component structure that consumes a JSON schema to render inputs. Include hooks for real-time validation and loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a reusable React Form component with Zod validation integration.'

steps.title: Implement Progressive Disclosure
steps.description: Control the user experience by revealing information only when necessary to reduce cognitive load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a logic layer for progressive disclosure in onboarding. The system should only unlock the next step based on the completion of the current step's required data points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement a conditional rendering layer that gates onboarding steps based on task completion.'

steps.title: Integrate Backend Persistence
steps.description: Ensure onboarding progress is saved to the database so users can resume without losing data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a backend service layer to persist onboarding progress. Include an endpoint to update current step and partial data payloads.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a Supabase/PostgreSQL migration for onboarding_progress table and create a corresponding CRUD service.'

steps.title: Add Analytics and Observability
steps.description: Track drop-off points to optimize your onboarding flow based on real user behavior.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up an analytics wrapper for the onboarding flow that logs step-completion events and time-to-complete metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add a PostHog tracking utility to the onboarding state machine to monitor drop-off rates.'

pro_tips: Never ask for more than three fields at once; use 'micro-commitments' to keep user momentum high.
Always include a 'Skip' option; forced onboarding is the fastest way to drive users to your competitors.
Use optimistic UI updates for your onboarding steps to make the app feel instantaneous, even during database writes.
Build a 'Progress Indicator' that shows the user exactly how far they are; humans are hardwired to finish incomplete tasks (Zeigarnik effect).
Test your onboarding flow on a mobile device first; if it's painful on a phone, it's already failed.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to Vercel, share the repository link in the community channel, and start looking for small SaaS founders who need their onboarding UX audited.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to solve the most critical problem in the SaaS industry. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the infrastructure that makes every idea successful. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
